# Academic Anchor: Manufactured Attention

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


Rogers and Righetti’s 2025 paper on coordinated inauthentic behaviour and manufactured attention is the most important academic source for the vault.

## Why it matters

The paper widens coordinated manipulation beyond classic political influence operations. It surfaces media groups, activist meme-sharing, advertising networks promoting gambling and cyber scams, and hijacked public groups spreading ads.

## Key contribution

The paper defines manufactured attention as artificial audience fabrication through coordinated behaviour. This maps directly onto the thesis that commercial systems can manufacture visibility and perceived demand.

## Use in paper

Use this source early to justify treating clipping networks, astroturfing, scam ads, and commercial amplification as related phenomena rather than isolated hacks.

## Source notes

- **Rogers & Righetti (2025) — Coordinated inauthentic behaviour on Facebook? A typology of manufactured attention** (Academic / open-access). Defines manufactured attention as artificial audience fabrication through coordinated behaviour; surfaces advertising networks, gambling/cyber-scam promotion, hijacked public groups, media groups, and political/activist coordination. URL: https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/29768624251369784
